questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。第一空根据yet（尚未）可知用现在完成时Have...done；第二空根据ago（……之前）可知用一般过去时finished。A项第一空用Did，与yet不匹配；C项第二空用have finished，与ago不匹配；D项时态全错。【技巧】yet是现在完成时标志词，ago是一般过去时标志词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl ______ is reading a book under the tree is my classmate.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选A。先行词是The girl（人），且定语从句中缺少主语，故用who。B项which指物；C项whom在从句中作宾语；D项whose表示'……的'，后需接名词。【技巧】先行词是人，从句缺主语用who，缺宾语用who/whom，表示所属用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the library tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。主语books与return之间是被动关系（书被归还），且时间状语是tomorrow，故用一般将来时的被动语态will be returned。A项是主动语态；C项是过去式，与tomorrow矛盾；D项是现在进行时的主动语态。【技巧】被动语态结构：be + 过去分词，注意时间状语决定时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Shanghai.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选C。根据'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海了，还没回来）可知，敲门的不可能是王先生，can't表示'不可能'，用于否定推测。A项must表示'一定是'，与句意矛盾；B、D项表示'可能'，不确定性，与语境不符。【技巧】情态动词表推测：must一定（肯定），can't不可能（否定），may/might可能（不确定）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We need to ______ the problem before we can solve it.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。find out指经过调查、研究后'查明，弄清楚'（真相、原因等），符合句意'我们需要先查明问题才能解决它'。A项look for强调寻找的动作；C项look after照顾；D项find强调找到的结果，不强调过程。【技巧】find out侧重'查明真相'，find侧重'找到东西'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The old man was ______ when he heard the good news.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选C。cheer up意为'高兴起来，振作起来'，符合语境'听到好消息时高兴起来'。A项look forward to期待（后需接名词/动名词）；B项break down出故障，崩溃；D项set off出发，引爆。【技巧】cheer up可分开使用，如cheer sb up使某人高兴。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】选C。un-是否定前缀，unhappy意为'令人不快的'；make sb + 形容词（使某人……），故第二空也用unhappy。A项第二空happily是副词；B项逻辑矛盾（好消息让人不开心）；D项第一空happily是副词，不能修饰news。【技巧】make + sb + adj.（形容词作宾补），un-前缀变否定。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】选A。此处需用现在完成时的被动语态has been，强调'已经被使用'且对现在造成影响（还能再利用）。B项is being是现在进行时的被动，表示正在被使用，与only used on one side（只用过一面）的完成状态不符；C项was一般过去时，无强调作用；D项will be将来时，与语境不符。【技巧】现在完成时被动语态：have/has been done，强调动作对现在的影响。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
The students in Class 8B are working on an environmental project this month. Their teacher, Mr. Li, asked them to come up with ideas to make their school greener.

Last Monday, the class had a heated discussion. "We should reduce waste first," said Tom. "Many students throw away paper that (8)______ only used on one side." Mary agreed and added, "We can also collect plastic bottles (9)______ are thrown in the bins and send them for recycling."

Then Lucy stood up and said, "I've (10)______ a better idea. Why don't we start a 'No Plastic Week'? Everyone can bring their own cups and lunch boxes instead of using disposable ones." Her classmates thought it was (11)______ practical way to cut down plastic waste.

The next day, they (12)______ up posters around the school to tell other students about their plan. To their surprise, over 200 students promised to join them.

questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。先行词是plastic bottles（物），定语从句中缺少主语，故用which引导。A项who指人；C项whom指人且作宾语；D项what不能引导定语从句。【技巧】先行词是物，从句缺主语或宾语时用which或that。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
The students in Class 8B are working on an environmental project this month. Their teacher, Mr. Li, asked them to come up with ideas to make their school greener.

Last Monday, the class had a heated discussion. "We should reduce waste first," said Tom. "Many students throw away paper that (8)______ only used on one side." Mary agreed and added, "We can also collect plastic bottles (9)______ are thrown in the bins and send them for recycling."

Then Lucy stood up and said, "I've (10)______ a better idea. Why don't we start a 'No Plastic Week'? Everyone can bring their own cups and lunch boxes instead of using disposable ones." Her classmates thought it was (11)______ practical way to cut down plastic waste.

The next day, they (12)______ up posters around the school to tell other students about their plan. To their surprise, over 200 students promised to join them.

questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。think of意为'想出，想到（主意、办法等）'，符合句意'我想出了一个更好的主意'。A项look for寻找；C项look after照顾；D项think about考虑，思考（过程），不强调结果。【技巧】think of强调'想出'结果，think about强调'思考'过程。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
The students in Class 8B are working on an environmental project this month. Their teacher, Mr. Li, asked them to come up with ideas to make their school greener.

Last Monday, the class had a heated discussion. "We should reduce waste first," said Tom. "Many students throw away paper that (8)______ only used on one side." Mary agreed and added, "We can also collect plastic bottles (9)______ are thrown in the bins and send them for recycling."

Then Lucy stood up and said, "I've (10)______ a better idea. Why don't we start a 'No Plastic Week'? Everyone can bring their own cups and lunch boxes instead of using disposable ones." Her classmates thought it was (11)______ practical way to cut down plastic waste.

The next day, they (12)______ up posters around the school to tell other students about their plan. To their surprise, over 200 students promised to join them.

questions.explanation: 【解析】选A。way是可数名词单数，此处表示'一种实用的方法'，是泛指；practical以辅音音素/p/开头，故用a。B项an用于元音音素开头；C项the表示特指；D项零冠词不符合语法。【技巧】不定冠词a/an表示'一……'，看发音不看字母，practical /ˈpræktɪkl/ 以辅音开头。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
The students in Class 8B are working on an environmental project this month. Their teacher, Mr. Li, asked them to come up with ideas to make their school greener.

Last Monday, the class had a heated discussion. "We should reduce waste first," said Tom. "Many students throw away paper that (8)______ only used on one side." Mary agreed and added, "We can also collect plastic bottles (9)______ are thrown in the bins and send them for recycling."

Then Lucy stood up and said, "I've (10)______ a better idea. Why don't we start a 'No Plastic Week'? Everyone can bring their own cups and lunch boxes instead of using disposable ones." Her classmates thought it was (11)______ practical way to cut down plastic waste.

The next day, they (12)______ up posters around the school to tell other students about their plan. To their surprise, over 200 students promised to join them.

questions.explanation: 【解析】选A。put up意为'张贴（海报、通知等）'，符合句意'他们在学校周围张贴海报'。B项give up放弃；C项turn up调大（音量）；D项take up占据，开始从事。【技巧】put up有'张贴'和'举起'等意思，结合posters（海报）选put up。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
The students in Class 8B are working on an environmental project this month. Their teacher, Mr. Li, asked them to come up with ideas to make their school greener.

Last Monday, the class had a heated discussion. "We should reduce waste first," said Tom. "Many students throw away paper that (8)______ only used on one side." Mary agreed and added, "We can also collect plastic bottles (9)______ are thrown in the bins and send them for recycling."

Then Lucy stood up and said, "I've (10)______ a better idea. Why don't we start a 'No Plastic Week'? Everyone can bring their own cups and lunch boxes instead of using disposable ones." Her classmates thought it was (11)______ practical way to cut down plastic waste.

The next day, they (12)______ up posters around the school to tell other students about their plan. To their surprise, over 200 students promised to join them.

questions.stem: How many hours do teenagers spend on screens daily according to the study?
questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。根据文章第一段'A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of seven hours a day on screens, not including school work.'可知，青少年平均每天花7小时在屏幕上（不含学业）。A项是睡前使用手机超过3小时的情况；C、D项文中未提及。【技巧】细节题要找准关键词study和hours，定位到第一段数字信息。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Do you spend hours staring at your smartphone every day? If so, you are not alone. A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of seven hours a day on screens, not including school work.

Dr. Sarah Chen, a scientist at Shenzhen University, has been studying the effects of screen time on young people's brains. "When we look at screens for too long, our brains produce dopamine, a chemical that makes us feel happy," she explains. "This is why social media apps are designed to keep us scrolling. They want us to stay on them as long as possible."

However, too much screen time can cause problems. The study found that students who use phones for more than three hours before bed often have trouble falling asleep. This is because the blue light from screens stops the body from producing melatonin, a hormone (荷尔蒙) that helps us sleep.

The good news is that small changes can make a big difference. Dr. Chen suggests the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest. She also recommends turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime.

"Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Chen says. "The key is to use it wisely and find a healthy balance between the online and offline worlds."

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Chen's words in the last paragraph?
options: A. We should never use smartphones.
B. Technology is harmful to teenagers.
C. We need to control our screen time properly.
D. Offline activities are more important than online ones.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选C。根据最后一段'Technology is not the enemy'和'find a healthy balance'可推断，Dr. Chen认为我们应该明智地使用技术，合理控制屏幕时间。A项'从不使用'与文意相反；B项'有害'与'不是敌人'矛盾；D项'线下比线上重要'过于绝对，文中说的是balance（平衡）。【技巧】推理题要基于文本，避免过度推断，balance是关键词。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Do you spend hours staring at your smartphone every day? If so, you are not alone. A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of seven hours a day on screens, not including school work.

Dr. Sarah Chen, a scientist at Shenzhen University, has been studying the effects of screen time on young people's brains. "When we look at screens for too long, our brains produce dopamine, a chemical that makes us feel happy," she explains. "This is why social media apps are designed to keep us scrolling. They want us to stay on them as long as possible."

However, too much screen time can cause problems. The study found that students who use phones for more than three hours before bed often have trouble falling asleep. This is because the blue light from screens stops the body from producing melatonin, a hormone (荷尔蒙) that helps us sleep.

The good news is that small changes can make a big difference. Dr. Chen suggests the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest. She also recommends turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime.

"Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Chen says. "The key is to use it wisely and find a healthy balance between the online and offline worlds."

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "scrolling" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
options: B. Moving content up and down on a screen.
D. Looking away from the screen.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。根据上下文'stay on them as long as possible'（让我们尽可能长时间停留在应用上）可知，scrolling是指在屏幕上滚动浏览内容。A项停止休息、C项关机、D项看向别处都与'停留'相反。【技巧】词义猜测题看上下文逻辑，'keep us scrolling'与'stay on them'同向，都是让用户持续使用。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Do you spend hours staring at your smartphone every day? If so, you are not alone. A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of seven hours a day on screens, not including school work.

Dr. Sarah Chen, a scientist at Shenzhen University, has been studying the effects of screen time on young people's brains. "When we look at screens for too long, our brains produce dopamine, a chemical that makes us feel happy," she explains. "This is why social media apps are designed to keep us scrolling. They want us to stay on them as long as possible."

However, too much screen time can cause problems. The study found that students who use phones for more than three hours before bed often have trouble falling asleep. This is because the blue light from screens stops the body from producing melatonin, a hormone (荷尔蒙) that helps us sleep.

The good news is that small changes can make a big difference. Dr. Chen suggests the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest. She also recommends turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime.

"Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Chen says. "The key is to use it wisely and find a healthy balance between the online and offline worlds."

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. How to Design Social Media Apps
C. Screen Time and Teenagers' Health
D. The Life of a Scientist
questions.explanation: 【解析】选C。文章主要讨论青少年屏幕使用时间（screen time）对健康的影响（睡眠、大脑等）及建议，C项最全面。A项智能手机历史未提及；B项设计应用只在第二句提及，非主旨；D项科学家生活偏离主题。【技巧】主旨题要覆盖全文，关键词screen time贯穿始终，health涵盖睡眠和大脑影响。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Do you spend hours staring at your smartphone every day? If so, you are not alone. A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of seven hours a day on screens, not including school work.

Dr. Sarah Chen, a scientist at Shenzhen University, has been studying the effects of screen time on young people's brains. "When we look at screens for too long, our brains produce dopamine, a chemical that makes us feel happy," she explains. "This is why social media apps are designed to keep us scrolling. They want us to stay on them as long as possible."

However, too much screen time can cause problems. The study found that students who use phones for more than three hours before bed often have trouble falling asleep. This is because the blue light from screens stops the body from producing melatonin, a hormone (荷尔蒙) that helps us sleep.

The good news is that small changes can make a big difference. Dr. Chen suggests the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest. She also recommends turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime.

"Technology is not the enemy," Dr. Chen says. "The key is to use it wisely and find a healthy balance between the online and offline worlds."

options: A. However, don't let your hands wave around too much.
B. Finally, review your performance before the actual speech.
C. Here is another important tip: know your material well.
D. The first step is to pick the right topic.
E. In addition, speak loudly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选D。根据下文'First, choose a topic...'（首先，选择话题）可知，此处应提出'第一步是选择合适的话题'，D项中的The first step与First对应，pick与choose对应。【技巧】注意段落衔接词First，前文应有总起句引出第一步。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Preparing for an English speech can be scary, but with the right steps, you can do it well. Here are some tips to help you.

(17)______ First, choose a topic that you are interested in. When you care about the subject, your passion will show in your voice and make the audience listen carefully.

(18)______ Don't just memorize every word. Instead, understand the main ideas and practice explaining them in your own words. This way, you won't panic if you forget a sentence.

Next, practice your body language. Stand up straight and make eye contact with your listeners. (19)______ Moving your hands naturally can also help emphasize important points.

(20)______ Try to record yourself and watch the video. You will notice small habits like saying "um" too often or looking down at your shoes. Fix these problems before the big day.

Remember, confidence comes from preparation. The more you practice, the better you will perform!

options: A. However, don't let your hands wave around too much.
B. Finally, review your performance before the actual speech.
C. Here is another important tip: know your material well.
D. The first step is to pick the right topic.
E. In addition, speak loudly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选C。根据下文'Don't just memorize...understand the main ideas'（不要死记硬背，要理解大意）可知，本段讲要熟悉演讲内容，C项'了解你的材料/内容'符合。A项讲手势、B项讲最后检查、D项讲选话题、E项讲大声说话均不符。【技巧】know your material与understand the main ideas同义复现。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Preparing for an English speech can be scary, but with the right steps, you can do it well. Here are some tips to help you.

(17)______ First, choose a topic that you are interested in. When you care about the subject, your passion will show in your voice and make the audience listen carefully.

(18)______ Don't just memorize every word. Instead, understand the main ideas and practice explaining them in your own words. This way, you won't panic if you forget a sentence.

Next, practice your body language. Stand up straight and make eye contact with your listeners. (19)______ Moving your hands naturally can also help emphasize important points.

(20)______ Try to record yourself and watch the video. You will notice small habits like saying "um" too often or looking down at your shoes. Fix these problems before the big day.

Remember, confidence comes from preparation. The more you practice, the better you will perform!

options: A. However, don't let your hands wave around too much.
B. Finally, review your performance before the actual speech.
C. Here is another important tip: know your material well.
D. The first step is to pick the right topic.
E. In addition, speak loudly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选E。本段讲肢体语言（body language），空前讲站姿和眼神交流，空后讲手势，E项'此外，大声清楚地说话'也是演讲技巧，与前后并列。A项However转折，与语境不符；B项Finally是最后一步，但本段开头是Next；C、D已选。【技巧】In addition表示递进补充，列举演讲技巧的不同方面。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Preparing for an English speech can be scary, but with the right steps, you can do it well. Here are some tips to help you.

(17)______ First, choose a topic that you are interested in. When you care about the subject, your passion will show in your voice and make the audience listen carefully.

(18)______ Don't just memorize every word. Instead, understand the main ideas and practice explaining them in your own words. This way, you won't panic if you forget a sentence.

Next, practice your body language. Stand up straight and make eye contact with your listeners. (19)______ Moving your hands naturally can also help emphasize important points.

(20)______ Try to record yourself and watch the video. You will notice small habits like saying "um" too often or looking down at your shoes. Fix these problems before the big day.

Remember, confidence comes from preparation. The more you practice, the better you will perform!

options: A. However, don't let your hands wave around too much.
B. Finally, review your performance before the actual speech.
C. Here is another important tip: know your material well.
D. The first step is to pick the right topic.
E. In addition, speak loudly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选B。根据下文'record yourself and watch the video'（录下自己并观看）可知，这是在演讲前检查/回顾自己的表现，且这是最后一步（前面有First, Next），B项'Finally'符合顺序。【技巧】Finally表示顺序最后，review performance与record yourself对应。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Preparing for an English speech can be scary, but with the right steps, you can do it well. Here are some tips to help you.

(17)______ First, choose a topic that you are interested in. When you care about the subject, your passion will show in your voice and make the audience listen carefully.

(18)______ Don't just memorize every word. Instead, understand the main ideas and practice explaining them in your own words. This way, you won't panic if you forget a sentence.

Next, practice your body language. Stand up straight and make eye contact with your listeners. (19)______ Moving your hands naturally can also help emphasize important points.

(20)______ Try to record yourself and watch the video. You will notice small habits like saying "um" too often or looking down at your shoes. Fix these problems before the big day.

Remember, confidence comes from preparation. The more you practice, the better you will perform!
